zarr_metadata.json_semantics: not JCS; the writer emits deterministic compact JSON while the reader accepts semantic JSON key order and insignificant whitespace
zarr_metadata.empty_optional_members: attributes and storage_transformers may be omitted or explicitly empty; the deterministic writer omits them
zarr_metadata.configuration_free_codec_spelling: crc32c is exactly a name-only object as required by the pinned Zarr 3.0 metadata grammar; string shorthand is rejected

fill_values.float32_pixels: JSON numeric positive zero; the deterministic writer emits 0.0

zarr_metadata.packed_index_dimension_names: the member is omitted and any present member is rejected
zarr_metadata.scope: empty Zarr groups, the nine storage-array rows, and the closed OME image-group DTO

ome_image_group.closed_members: unknown or duplicate members are rejected at the group, attributes, ome, multiscale, axis, dataset, and transform levels
ome_image_group.namespace: attributes contains only ome; ome contains version=0.5 and exactly one multiscales item; omero is forbidden
ome_image_group.multiscale: contains exactly axes and one through seven datasets ordered by relative path s00 through s06
ome_image_group.axes: exactly ordered t,c,z,y,x; regular time carries second; nonregular time omits unit; diagonal spatial axes carry micrometer; arbitrary-affine projection omits spatial units
ome_image_group.transforms: each dataset contains one five-value scale followed by at most one five-value translation; channel scale is one; time and channel translations are positive zero; time and channel are never downsampled
ome_image_group.spatial_projection: diagonal geometry stores exact micrometer scale/translation; arbitrary affine stores unitless identity spatial transforms and remains M4D-owned
ome_image_group.translation_omission: translation is omitted exactly when all five values are positive zero
ome_image_group.number_semantics: the reader accepts finite semantic JSON numbers but rejects stored negative zero; the writer normalizes zero and emits one deterministic compact representation
ome_image_group.official_schema_boundary: offline pinned OME schema validation and independent readback remain package-validator and T1 evidence gates

normalization.transform_negative_zero: normalize to positive zero before encoding; a stored negative-zero spelling is noncanonical

cross_object_boundary.display_object_local_rule: layers are strictly increasing and unique
cross_object_boundary.package_rule: profile, science, and display must have the same nonempty contiguous logical-layer ordinals

recipe_graph.input_uniqueness: unique by the ordered pair role then node

package_manifest.path_ordinals: image 0 through 3 and scale 0 through 6; shard coordinates use canonical unsigned decimal components
package_manifest.page_entries: nonempty and strictly sorted unique by ASCII package path
package_manifest.page_packing: path-sort descriptors, then greedily append the next descriptor iff the exact canonical page remains within the byte limit
package_manifest.root_pages: one through six contiguous p######## paths from zero, positive counts and byte lengths, and strictly increasing nonoverlapping path bounds
package_manifest.cross_page_verification: each reference matches exact count, bounds, bytes, and digest and flattening then repacking reproduces the identical greedy pages
package_manifest.root_package_id: SHA-256 of the exact canonical root bytes; package_id is not stored inside the root

local_metadata_catalog.entry_order: parse the bounded profile, then the canonical manifest root and all one through six authenticated manifest pages
local_metadata_catalog.verified_objects: verify exact bytes and parse the profile, science, display, empty Zarr groups, image-group OME metadata, and every profile-addressed Zarr array metadata object
local_metadata_catalog.portable_record_policy: profile paths and manifest descriptor roles are checked during open; portable record bytes remain lazy
local_metadata_catalog.descriptor_set: every non-shard descriptor must be exactly one profile-derived required metadata/control path; shard descriptors remain authenticated claims but their files are not read
local_metadata_catalog.cross_object_checks: profile/science/display identity and layer sets match; physical channels exactly cover c; OME level/time/base geometry and IO-2 eligibility match science; co-grouped layers share science grids; per-level pixel dtype and ceil-divided shapes, validity t,c,z,y,ceil(x/8), packed-index logical-brick counts, and packed-coordinate u32 capacity match
local_metadata_catalog.reported_identity: the root digest is exposed only as the declared PackageId until full closure validation proves every descriptor payload digest
local_metadata_catalog.reported_metric: exact metadata bytes read is retained for the later metadata-open evidence gate
local_metadata_catalog.non_claims: open does not enumerate directory closure, verify shard bytes, decode indexes, compute scientific identity, or imply full package validation

local_directory_inventory.invocation: a separate explicit operation after metadata open; never implicit in open
local_directory_inventory.file_set: exactly all manifest descriptor paths, the manifest root, and every root-referenced manifest page
local_directory_inventory.directory_set: exactly the package root and the ancestor directories implied by the finalized file set; unlisted empty directories are rejected
local_directory_inventory.filesystem_types: do not follow links; reject symlinks, hardlinked regular files, non-regular objects, root escape, and directory identity drift
local_directory_inventory.authority_stability: manifest root and page authority bytes are reauthenticated before and after enumeration; files and directories receive a final identity/length sweep
local_directory_inventory.lengths: every regular file length must equal its authenticated descriptor or manifest-page/root fact; shard payload digests remain full-validation work
local_directory_inventory.counting: root counts as a directory; report maximum directory depth; fan-out counts direct child files plus direct child directories
local_directory_inventory.bounds: stop above the global DS-4 maxima of 11264 regular files, 14336 directories, or 512 direct children; DS-specific admission remains later validator work
local_directory_inventory.cancellation: the caller-supplied cancellation check is polled before and throughout enumeration
local_directory_inventory.profile_selection: the inventory does not select or infer DS-0 through DS-4

dataset_profile_admission.selection: the caller explicitly selects DS-0 through DS-4 for one validation or evidence scenario; the package does not store a DS label and the implementation never infers a smallest fitting profile
dataset_profile_admission.scale_rule: scale limits apply independently to every image; the report records the maximum scales per image, while exact-profile admission requires every image to have the exact count
dataset_profile_admission.logical_counts: derive logical S0 bytes from checked scientific-layer t,z,y,x and dtype facts and logical bricks from pixel-array shape divided by the frozen inner chunk grid without enumerating bricks
dataset_profile_admission.shard_counts: derive addressed pixel, explicit-validity, and packed-index shards arithmetically; count actual shard descriptors separately from the exact directory inventory
dataset_profile_admission.descriptor_bounds: every shard descriptor must belong to one profile-addressed array and have in-range outer coordinates; pixel and validity descriptors may be subsets, while packed-index descriptors require exact coverage
dataset_profile_admission.elision_boundary: zero actual pixel shards may pass when addressed pixel shards are positive; admission only records provisional elision and does not authorize omitted payloads before packed-record structural validation
dataset_profile_admission.ceilings: apply the selected profile to logical bytes/bricks, addressed shard counts, metadata/pages, actual physical objects, directories, depth, and fan-out with checked arithmetic
dataset_profile_admission.cancellation: poll before and throughout the fresh inventory, image/level arithmetic, descriptor pass, and final admission
dataset_profile_admission.non_claims: admission is a bounded count/address check, not DS fixture qualification, shard-byte integrity, structural/full validation, scientific identity, PackageId-attributed payload authority, or product support

local_brick_address_plan.input: one PackedIndexCoordinates value in image,scale,t,c,z,y,x logical-brick coordinates
local_brick_address_plan.bounds: image, scale, and every brick coordinate are checked against the catalog's cross-validated pixel shape before path construction
local_brick_address_plan.pixel_and_validity: derive default-key shard paths as array/c/t/c/z_outer/y_outer/x_outer with four inner chunks per spatial shard axis and lexicographic x-fastest inner slots
local_brick_address_plan.packed_index: derive the per-image-scale C-order record ordinal, indexes/.../c/floor(record/16384)/0 shard, floor((record mod 16384)/256) inner slot, and 64*(record mod 256) byte offset
local_brick_address_plan.edge_extent: report the exact nonpadded z,y,x extent for boundary bricks
local_brick_address_plan.descriptor_policy: the packed-index shard descriptor is mandatory; pixel and explicit-validity shard descriptors may be absent until the packed record proves fill elision
local_brick_address_plan.non_claims: planning reads no shard bytes and proves no payload CRC, shard digest, packed record, or scientific value

local_brick_read.input: one descriptor-derived LocalBrickAddressPlan from an authenticated local metadata catalog
local_brick_read.exposure: crate-private precursor only until full manifest SHA-256 validation can issue an explicit package-integrity capability
local_brick_read.range_io: for each selected component, open the checked shard once, read and validate its exact fixed end-index tail, then read at most the selected encoded inner-payload range through the same file handle
local_brick_read.selection_order: read the mandatory packed-index component first; read pixels only when the validated packed record declares a pixel payload; read explicit validity only when the validated record has a positive valid-voxel count
local_brick_read.integrity: require exact manifest-declared object length, canonical zero-slack shard index structure, CRC32C for the index and each selected inner payload, bounded zstd output, requested packed-record coordinates, and profile-consistent validity mode
local_brick_read.elision: a missing pixel inner payload is accepted only when the packed record validates fill elision; a missing explicit-validity payload is accepted only for an all-invalid record; every selected required inner payload must be present
local_brick_read.result: return the validated packed record, exact nonpadded edge extent, optional fixed-capacity decoded pixel and validity payloads, and storage-boundary range/read/decode counters
local_brick_read.amplification: enforce the frozen absolute cold-request, encoded-read-byte, and decoded-byte ceilings for regular and fully elided one-brick reads
local_brick_read.authority_stability: file device, inode, length, mtime, and ctime are checked around each component read and every used shard receives one final bounded identity sweep
local_brick_read.non_claims: returned bytes cannot be attributed to the catalog PackageId; the operation does not verify whole-object manifest SHA-256, perform DS-specific admission or complete-package validation, compute scientific identity, or establish product support

package_structural_reconciliation.exposure: crate-private precursor composed after explicit DS admission; it issues no public validation report or read capability
package_structural_reconciliation.packed_index_scan: read each bounded packed-index shard coherently once, verify its descriptor length and SHA-256, canonical tail and inner CRC32C, decode exactly the logical records in C-order, and require zero array-fill bytes after the final logical record in its decoded inner chunk
package_structural_reconciliation.record_checks: each record must contain its exact image, scale, t, c, z, y, x coordinates; validate statistics against the exact nonpadded edge-brick capacity and require the profile's validity mode
package_structural_reconciliation.canonical_slots: required packed-index inner slots are present and all unused outer slots are missing; pixel inner-slot presence is exactly pixel_payload_present; explicit-validity inner-slot presence is exactly valid_voxel_count greater than zero; every out-of-grid slot is missing
package_structural_reconciliation.empty_shards: a listed pixel or validity shard must contain at least one record-required inner payload; all-missing physical shard objects are noncanonical
package_structural_reconciliation.bounds: run in bounded O((logical records plus addressed shard slots) times log addressed shards), retain only per-shard bitmasks, object snapshots, and one packed-index shard, use checked counters, and poll cancellation throughout
package_structural_reconciliation.final_recheck: repeat the exact directory and manifest-authority inspection after reconciliation, then revalidate every inspected shard snapshot as the last success gate; detected mutation or cancellation fails the attempt
package_structural_reconciliation.non_claims: reconciliation does not hash pixel or validity shard payloads, recompute their scientific statistics, prove the declared PackageId closure, authorize PackageId-attributed reads, compute ScientificContentId, establish IO-3, qualify T1, activate the product, or freeze a stable format

exact_package_validation.entry: consume one authenticated local metadata catalog after explicit caller-selected DS admission; no capability may be paired with another root or coexist with an unverified catalog read path
exact_package_validation.sequence: complete package-wide structural reconciliation first, then hash the manifest root, every referenced manifest page, and every descriptor object in deterministic order, repeat the exact directory and manifest-authority inspection, and finish with one cancellable ordered snapshot sweep before capability issuance
exact_package_validation.hashing: stream each object through raw SHA-256 using one open handle, exact declared length, one fixed 65536-byte buffer, cancellation before and throughout reads, and same-open identity checks; retain no payload bytes
exact_package_validation.phase_coherence: every shard snapshot observed by structural reconciliation must exactly equal the corresponding full-hash snapshot, preventing structure from version A and digest from version B from being combined
exact_package_validation.capability: issue one owning non-clone exact-package capability binding the proved PackageId, caller-selected DS admission, the complete closure observed by validation, manifest root/page snapshots, descriptor-aligned object snapshots, and checked object/byte counters
exact_package_validation.attributed_reads: PackageId-attributed brick reads exist only on the exact-package capability; each read revalidates manifest authority and proves every actually consumed shard is the same object captured by full validation before returning CRC-checked bounded payloads
exact_package_validation.freshness: the final sweep and explicit complete revalidation are sequential best-effort mutation checks, not an atomic filesystem snapshot; normal brick reads do not rescan unrelated objects and instead revalidate current authority and every consumed object before returning attributed bytes
exact_package_validation.bounds: validation is O(total finalized object bytes plus structural work plus finalized objects times log finalized objects), retains O(finalized physical objects) bounded snapshots and paths plus one 65536-byte hash buffer, accumulates checked counters, and polls cancellation through every phase
exact_package_validation.non_claims: exact package validation does not create an atomic filesystem snapshot or lock out concurrent writers, parse lazy portable-record semantics, recompute ScientificContentId or pixel statistics, establish official OME schema conformance or IO-3, qualify independent T1, implement a writer or publication, activate the product, prove post-issuance immutability of unrelated files on every brick read, make a product-open or performance claim, or freeze a stable format

deterministic_package_writer.entry: WP-10A-B create-only off-product writer, bound to foundation-wp-08b-exit-1 and the accepted profile freeze; rollback is one writer checkpoint with no product authority flip
deterministic_package_writer.input: typed profile, science, display, portable-record, OME, and Zarr facts plus a lazy sequence of decoded shard inputs; callers cannot supply encoded shards, object descriptors, manifest pages, manifest roots, or package paths outside the typed profile
deterministic_package_writer.ownership: the writer derives canonical control and metadata bytes, default-key shard paths, deterministic zstd/CRC32C shard bytes, exact object descriptors, greedy pages, root bytes, and PackageId
deterministic_package_writer.bounds: consume one outer-shard input at a time, retain at most that profile-bounded decoded shard, one encoded inner payload, one fixed shard index, and the DS-4-bounded descriptor/page set; poll cancellation throughout encoding, writing, validation, synchronization, and immediately before publication
deterministic_package_writer.canonicality: input enumeration order cannot affect final paths or bytes; omit all-missing pixel and validity shards, require packed-index coverage through structural reconciliation, and emit lexicographic zero-slack shard payloads
deterministic_package_writer.staging: create one private mode-0700 sibling directory on the destination filesystem, create every file once, write payloads before manifest pages and the root, hash exact bytes while writing, then sync every file and created directory
deterministic_package_writer.validation: open a fresh catalog, perform the caller-selected DS admission, structural reconciliation, exact inventory, and final writer-snapshot sweep before commit; do not repeat a whole-payload hash scan because write-time hashing already produced every descriptor digest
deterministic_package_writer.publication: the destination must be absent; publish with Linux renameat2 RENAME_NOREPLACE in the already-open parent directory and then sync that parent; no existing package is replaced in this slice
deterministic_package_writer.failure: failure or cancellation before rename leaves the destination absent and performs fd-held best-effort cleanup of the owned stage; a parent-sync failure after rename returns a typed commit-indeterminate error and never removes the valid published package
deterministic_package_writer.local_threat_model: mode-0700 staging, fd-relative no-follow creation, inode checks, and RENAME_NOREPLACE protect normal operation and accidental destination races; any hostile actor able to mutate the destination-parent namespace is outside this academic local-writer claim
deterministic_package_writer.source_safety: the API accepts no mutable source path and publication never edits, renames, or deletes source microscopy data
deterministic_package_writer.evidence: prove byte-identical output and PackageId across different parents and input order, exact open/admission/structural/full-read compatibility, fill and explicit-validity edges, cancellation cleanup, injected pre/post-commit failures, destination collision and symlink safety, bounds, and source immutability
deterministic_package_writer.non_claims: no TIFF/import pipeline, multiscale generation, scientific-ID computation, replacement, resume journal, product wiring, IO-3, independent T1, release packaging, remote/archive store, performance qualification, product-open validation, or stable-format promise

portable_records.scope: initial package-contained version-1 portable records; adding another source or license literal is a hard schema change
portable_records.record_ordinals: zero through thirteen and equal to the ordinal in m4d/records/r########.json
portable_records.subject_id_order: strictly sorted unique by exact ASCII typed-id spelling
portable_records.nfc_set_order: strictly sorted unique by normalized UTF-8 bytes
portable_records.source_identifier_order: strictly sorted unique by scheme ASCII bytes then value UTF-8 bytes

source_schemes.sha256: exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal digits for the source object's raw bytes

portable_records.doi: lowercase ASCII, at most 255 bytes, 10. then 4 through 9 digits then / then one or more [a-z0-9._;()/:-] characters; no URL or doi: prefix
portable_records.citation: title and creators are nonempty NFC text; creators preserve semantic order and may repeat; year is null or 1000 through 9999; DOI is independently nullable

derivation_record.scope_kind: package-contained m4d-portable-record derivation payload; external packaging records are a separate future schema
derivation_record.binding_order: strictly sorted unique by role ASCII bytes then exact typed-id ASCII bytes
derivation_record.input_id_families: all version-1 TypedId variants; later recipe and package validation owns semantic role compatibility
derivation_record.output_id_families: all version-1 TypedId variants in the base derivation body
derivation_record.scope: CanonicalValue map with exactly layers, space, and time entries in canonical key order
derivation_record.scope_layers: nonempty sorted unique list of u64 values no greater than u32 maximum
derivation_record.scope_time: nonempty list of maps with exactly end and start u64 entries; inclusive ranges require start no greater than end and are sorted by start then end and nonoverlapping
derivation_record.scope_space: one through 256 maps with exactly extent_tzyx and origin_tzyx; each is four u64 values, extents are positive, checked exclusive ends fit u64, boxes are sorted by origin then extent, and every pair is nonoverlapping as half-open 4D boxes
derivation_record.implementation: exactly name and version ASCII tokens plus build as the raw SHA-256 digest of the immutable implementation artifact named by the operation registry
derivation_record.coupling: input and output arrays may be empty; outcome and exactness do not silently invent or discard bindings
derivation_record.package_cycle: the m4d-portable-record wrapper rejects derivation outputs containing PackageId or ReleaseId; an external packaging record may use the base body to name its resulting PackageId without entering that package closure

release_record.dataset_series_uuid: lowercase hyphenated RFC 4122 UUID with version 4 and RFC 4122 variant bits
release_record.release_ordinal: positive u64 decimal; a later catalog validator requires increasing ordinals within one dataset series
release_record.ascii_id_sets: strictly sorted unique by exact ASCII spelling
release_record.nfc_sets: strictly sorted unique by normalized UTF-8 bytes
release_record.portable_record_digests: sorted unique and possibly empty exact-byte digests
release_record.citation: exactly title, year, and doi using the portable citation scalar rules
release_record.creators: nonempty semantic-order NFC list; repeated equal names are permitted
release_record.evidence: possibly empty entries of exactly kind and digest, strictly sorted unique by kind then digest
release_record.published_at: exact YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ UTC Gregorian timestamp, year 0001 through 9999, valid month/day and leap year, seconds 00 through 59
release_record.supersedes: possibly empty sorted unique ReleaseId set; the envelope rejects its own ReleaseId
release_record.cross_release_validation: a later catalog validator requires every superseded release to use the same dataset series and a lower ordinal
